sourceText: Forging military alliances, creating a constitution, and establishing the Golden Stool as a unifying symbol of divine authority.
translatedText: Ƙirƙirar ƙawance na soja, ƙirƙirar kundin tsarin mulki, da kafa Golden Stool a matsayin alamar haɗin kai na ikon Allah.

sourceText: Late 17th - Early 18th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 17th - Farkon ƙarni na 18

sourceText: Olaudah Equiano
translatedText: Olaudah Equiano

sourceText: Kidnapped from my home in Africa, I endured the unimaginable brutality of the Middle Passage and the dehumanization of slavery.
translatedText: An sace ni daga gidana a Afirka, na jure rashin tausayi mara misaltuwa na Tsakanin Tsakiya da kuma wulakanta bauta.

sourceText: Through diligence, trade, and a profound faith, I purchased my freedom and used the power of my own story to fight for the abolition of the slave trade.
translatedText: Ta wurin himma, kasuwanci, da bangaskiya mai zurfi, na sayi ’yanci kuma na yi amfani da ikon labarina don yaƙi don kawar da cinikin bayi.

sourceText: 18th Century
translatedText: Karni na 18

sourceText: Yohannes IV
translatedText: Yohanna IV

sourceText: Constant foreign invasions and internal rivalries. My reign was a perpetual state of war against Egyptians, Italians, and the Mahdists of Sudan.
translatedText: Rikicin kasashen waje na dindindin da kishiyoyin ciki. Mulkina ya kasance na dindindin na yaƙi da Masarawa, Italiyawa, da Mahdist na Sudan.

sourceText: Through military prowess and shrewd diplomacy, I preserved Ethiopia's sovereignty and forged a stronger national identity rooted in our shared Orthodox faith.
translatedText: Ta hanyar bajintar soja da wayo na diflomasiyya, na kiyaye ikon Habasha kuma na ƙirƙiri ƙaƙƙarfan asalin ƙasa mai tushe a cikin bangaskiyarmu ta Orthodox.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Witnessing the corruption, syncretic religious practices, and unjust rule of the Hausa city-states, which led to persecution and exile for my community.
translatedText: Shaidawa da cin hanci da rashawa, ayyukan addini da suka yi daidai da kama-karya, da mulkin rashin adalci da aka yi a garuruwan Hausawa, wanda ya jawo fitina da gudun hijira ga al’ummata.

sourceText: Leading a successful jihad (the Fulani War) against the Hausa rulers and establishing the Sokoto Caliphate, a new state founded on Islamic law and principles of social justice.
translatedText: Jagoranci nasarar jihadi (Yakin Fulani) akan sarakunan Hausawa da kafa Daular Sokoto, sabuwar kasa wacce aka kafa bisa shari'ar Musulunci da ka'idojin adalci na zamantakewa.

sourceText: Late 18th - Early 19th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 18th - Farkon Ƙarni na 19

sourceText: The relentless encroachment of Portuguese slavers, internal betrayals, and the personal loss of her family to the conflict.
translatedText: Ƙullawar barayi na Portugal ba tare da ɓata lokaci ba, cin amana na ciki, da asarar danginta na sirri ga rikici.

sourceText: Through brilliant military alliances, masterful diplomacy, and an unwavering resolve to protect her people's sovereignty.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ƙwararrun ƙawance na soja, ƙwararrun diflomasiyya, da yunƙurin kare ƴancin jama'arta.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: Idris Alooma
translatedText: Idris Alooma

sourceText: Inheriting a fractured empire, facing threats from neighboring groups, and commanding an outdated army.
translatedText: Gadon daular da ta wargaje, fuskantar barazana daga ƙungiyoyin maƙwabta, da kuma ba da umarni ga tsofaffin sojoji.

sourceText: By modernizing the military with Ottoman firearms, centralizing administration under Islamic law, and conducting strategic campaigns to unify and expand the empire.
translatedText: Ta hanyar zamanantar da sojoji da makaman Ottoman, sanya tsarin mulki a karkashin tsarin shari'ar Musulunci, da gudanar da dabarun yakin neman hadewa da fadada daular.

sourceText: Late 16th - Early 17th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 16 - Farkon Ƙarni na 17

sourceText: As a commoner by birth, I had to navigate the rigid hierarchy of the royal court and prove my worth through sheer intellect and skill.
translatedText: A matsayina na kowa a haihuwa, dole ne in yi tafiya a kan tsattsauran matsayi na gidan sarauta kuma in tabbatar da kimara ta hanyar basira da fasaha.

sourceText: My service to the Pharaoh was so valued that I was granted honors typically reserved for royalty, eventually being deified as a god of medicine and wisdom.
translatedText: Hidima na ga Fir’auna ya kasance mai daraja sosai har aka ba ni girma da aka keɓe don sarauta, daga ƙarshe aka mai da ni allahn magani da hikima.

sourceText: Old Kingdom of Egypt (c. 27th Century BCE)
translatedText: Tsohuwar Mulkin Masar (C. karni na 27 KZ)

sourceText: Witnessing the religious laxity and anthropomorphism prevalent under the Almoravid dynasty.
translatedText: Shaidar laxity na addini da anthropomorphism da ke yaɗu a ƙarƙashin daular Almoravid.

sourceText: By formulating a strict doctrine of divine unity (Tawhid) and uniting Berber tribes into a powerful religio-political force.
translatedText: Ta hanyar tsara ƙaƙƙarfan koyarwar haɗin kai na Allah (Tawhid) da haɗa ƙabilun Berber su zama wani ƙarfi na addini da siyasa.

sourceText: 12th Century
translatedText: Karni na 12

sourceText: Facing the constant threat of war, the challenge of being a female ruler in a patriarchal era, and the burden of defending and expanding her kingdom.
translatedText: Fuskantar barazanar yaƙi akai-akai, ƙalubalen kasancewarta mace mai mulki a zamanin uba, da nauyin karewa da faɗaɗa mulkinta.

sourceText: Through unmatched military skill, strategic genius, and inspiring leadership, she not only defended her kingdom but significantly expanded its influence and wealth.
translatedText: Ta hanyar fasahar soja da ba ta misaltuwa, hazakar dabara, da jagoranci mai ban sha'awa, ba wai kawai ta kare mulkinta ba amma ta fadada tasirinta da dukiyarta.

sourceText: Late 16th Century
translatedText: Marigayi Karni na 16

sourceText: Forced into solitude, hiding in a cave for two years to escape religious persecution.
translatedText: An tilastawa zama kadaici, yana ɓoye a cikin kogo na tsawon shekaru biyu don guje wa tsanantawar addini.

sourceText: Used his isolation to develop a philosophy of reason, concluding all humans are equal and recording his thoughts in the 'Hatata'.
translatedText: Ya yi amfani da keɓantacce don haɓaka falsafar hankali, kammala duk mutane daidai suke da kuma rubuta tunaninsa a cikin 'Hatata'.

sourceText: 17th Century
translatedText: Karni na 17

sourceText: John Chilembwe
translatedText: John Chilembwe

sourceText: The oppression and racial injustice of colonial rule.
translatedText: Zalunci da rashin adalci na launin fata na mulkin mallaka.

sourceText: Building resistance through education, faith, and ultimately, armed rebellion.
translatedText: Gina juriya ta hanyar ilimi, imani, kuma daga ƙarshe, tawaye da makamai.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Cetshwayo kaMpande
translatedText: Nasihar Mpande

sourceText: Losing my kingdom and the independence of my people to British imperial expansion and internal strife.
translatedText: Rasa mulkina da 'yancin kai na jama'ata zuwa fadada mulkin mallaka na Burtaniya da rikicin cikin gida.

sourceText: I traveled to London to plead my case to Queen Victoria, successfully arguing for my restoration to the throne, albeit with diminished power.
translatedText: Na yi tattaki zuwa Landan don neman karar ta ga Sarauniya Victoria, inda na yi nasarar yin gardama kan maidota kan karagar mulki, duk da cewa karfinsa ya ragu.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Being posthumously depicted as a cruel tyrant by later historians like Herodotus, and having the true purpose of my pyramid misunderstood.
translatedText: Kasancewa bayan mutuwa da aka kwatanta a matsayin azzalumi azzalumi daga baya masana tarihi kamar Herodotus, da kuma samun ainihin manufar dala tawa.

sourceText: The Great Pyramid itself stands as the ultimate testament to my organizational power, the prosperity of my kingdom, and our unshakeable faith in the eternal afterlife.
translatedText: Babban dala da kansa ya tsaya a matsayin madaidaicin shaida ga ikon kungiyata, wadatar mulkina, da bangaskiyarmu mara girgiza cikin lahira ta har abada.

sourceText: Ancient Egypt, 4th Dynasty
translatedText: Tsohon Misira, Daular Hudu

sourceText: Constant internal power struggles with my own family for the throne and the ever-present threat of Roman annexation looming over my kingdom.
translatedText: Ƙarfin cikin gida na dindindin yana kokawa tare da iyalina don sarauta da kuma barazanar mamaye Romawa da ke neman mamaye masarautata.

sourceText: By forging powerful personal and political alliances with Rome's most influential leaders, Julius Caesar and Mark Antony, I secured my throne and prolonged Egypt's independence.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ƙulla ƙawance mai ƙarfi na sirri da na siyasa tare da manyan shugabannin Roma, Julius Kaisar da Mark Antony, na sami gadon sarauta na kuma na tsawaita ’yancin kai na Masar.

sourceText: Ptolemaic Period
translatedText: Lokacin Ptolemaic

sourceText: A homeland torn apart by civil war, the hypocrisy of European missionaries, and the conflict between traditional beliefs and Christianity.
translatedText: Ƙasar mahaifa da yaƙin basasa ya wargaje, munafuncin ƴan mishan na Turai, da kuma rikici tsakanin imani na gargajiya da Kiristanci.

sourceText: Forging a new religious movement from visions of Saint Anthony, uniting the Kongo people to reoccupy and rebuild their ancient capital.
translatedText: Ƙirƙirar wani sabon motsi na addini daga hangen nesa na Saint Anthony, haɗa kan mutanen Kongo don sake mamayewa da sake gina tsohuwar babban birninsu.

sourceText: Late 17th - Early 18th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 17th - Farkon ƙarni na 18

sourceText: Tariq ibn Ziyad
translatedText: Tariq bin Ziyad

sourceText: Conflict and jealousy from his superior, Musa ibn Nusayr, and being recalled to Damascus, where he lived out his days in obscurity.
translatedText: Rikici da hassada daga babbansa, Musa bn Nusayr, da kuma tuno da shi zuwa Dimashƙu, inda ya yi rayuwarsa a cikin duhu.

sourceText: His military genius and bold leadership were immortalized in history, and the name 'Gibraltar' ensures his legacy endures.
translatedText: Hazakar sojansa da jajircewar shugabancinsa sun kasance dawwama cikin tarihi, kuma sunan 'Gibraltar' yana tabbatar da cewa gadonsa ya dawwama.

sourceText: Early 8th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 8

sourceText: Taytu Betul
translatedText: Haka ne

sourceText: The colonial ambitions of Italy and the constant threat from internal political rivals.
translatedText: Burin mulkin mallaka na Italiya da kuma ci gaba da barazana daga abokan hamayyar siyasa na cikin gida.

sourceText: Defended Ethiopia's sovereignty through brilliant diplomacy, military insight, and a strong partnership with her husband, Emperor Menelik II.
translatedText: Ta kare ikon Habasha ta hanyar diflomasiyya mai haske, basirar soja, da kuma kawance mai karfi da mijinta, Emperor Menelik II.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Abubakar Tafawa Balewa
translatedText: Abubakar Tafawa Balewa

sourceText: Navigating intense regional and ethnic rivalries while building a new nation.
translatedText: Gudanar da zazzafar hamayyar yanki da kabilanci yayin gina sabuwar al'umma.

sourceText: Through diplomacy, compromise, and advocating for a federal system to accommodate diversity.
translatedText: Ta hanyar diflomasiyya, sasantawa, da bayar da shawarwari ga tsarin tarayya don ɗaukar bambancin ra'ayi.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karni na 20

sourceText: The relentless invasions by the Assyrian Empire, which ultimately forced my retreat from Lower Egypt.
translatedText: Mamayewar da Daular Assuriya ta yi, wanda a ƙarshe ya tilasta ni ja da baya daga Ƙasar Masar.

sourceText: By withdrawing to my Nubian homeland, I preserved the heart of the Kushite kingdom and ensured its culture would endure.
translatedText: Ta hanyar janyewa zuwa mahaifata ta Nubian, na adana zuciyar masarautar Kushite kuma na tabbatar da al'adunta za su dore.

sourceText: 7th Century BCE
translatedText: Karni na 7 KZ

sourceText: Witnessing the moral corruption of Roman society and the brutal, unjust persecution of fellow Christians.
translatedText: Shaidar gurɓacewar ɗabi’a na al’ummar Romawa da kuma zalunci na rashin adalci da ake yi wa ’yan’uwa Kiristoci.

sourceText: Through prolific writing, he defended Christianity (apologetics) and forged a new Latin theological vocabulary to articulate and preserve the faith.
translatedText: Ta hanyar rubuce-rubuce masu yawa, ya kare Kiristanci (apologetics) kuma ya ƙirƙira sabon ƙamus na tauhidin Latin don bayyanawa da kiyaye bangaskiya.

sourceText: Roman Empire
translatedText: Daular Rum

sourceText: Tewodros II
translatedText: Theodore II

sourceText: A fractured empire and encroaching foreign powers.
translatedText: Daular da ta karye da mamaye kasashen waje.

sourceText: Reunification through military conquest and centralizing reforms.
translatedText: Haɗuwa ta hanyar mamaye sojoji da daidaita gyare-gyare.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Tutankhamun
translatedText: Tutankhamun

sourceText: Ascending the throne as a child amidst religious turmoil, I was burdened by political intrigue and suffered from numerous physical ailments throughout my short life.
translatedText: Da na hau kan karagar mulki sa’ad da nake yaro a cikin rikice-rikice na addini, na yi fama da ɓatanci na siyasa kuma na sha fama da cututtuka da yawa a rayuwata.

sourceText: I worked to restore stability by reversing my father Akhenaten's radical monotheistic reforms and re-establishing the traditional pantheon of Egyptian gods.
translatedText: Na yi aiki don maido da kwanciyar hankali ta hanyar juyar da sauye-sauye na tauhidi na mahaifina Akhenaton da sake kafa al'adun gargajiya na allolin Masar.

sourceText: New Kingdom, 18th Dynasty
translatedText: Sabuwar Mulki, Daular 18th

sourceText: The pressure of British imperialism, the division of my kingdom, and a long 28-year exile.
translatedText: Matsin mulkin daular Birtaniyya, da raba mulkina, da gudun hijira na tsawon shekaru 28.

sourceText: Returning from exile to a hero's welcome, being restored as Kumasihene, and becoming a symbol of Ashanti unity and identity.
translatedText: Dawowa daga gudun hijira zuwa maraba da jarumi, an maido da shi a matsayin Kumasihene, kuma ya zama alamar Ashanti haɗin kai da ainihi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: The political fragmentation of Egypt and the rise of ambitious local rulers who threatened traditional religious practices.
translatedText: Rarrabuwar siyasa ta Masar da haɓakar manyan sarakunan cikin gida waɗanda ke yin barazana ga ayyukan addini na gargajiya.

sourceText: By launching a holy war in the name of Amun, defeating the northern coalition, and reunifying Egypt under his Kushite rule.
translatedText: Ta hanyar kaddamar da yaki mai tsarki da sunan Amun, da fatattakar kawancen arewa, da hade Masar karkashin mulkinsa na Kushi.

sourceText: Third Intermediate Period of Egypt
translatedText: Tsakanin Tsakanin Lokaci na Uku na Masar

sourceText: Herbert Macaulay
translatedText: Herbert Macaulay

sourceText: Endured constant surveillance, multiple imprisonments by the colonial government, and faced opposition and misunderstanding from some of his own countrymen.
translatedText: Ya ci gaba da sa ido akai-akai, da dauri da yawa daga gwamnatin mulkin mallaka, da kuma fuskantar adawa da rashin fahimta daga wasu 'yan kasarsa.

sourceText: Persisted by founding newspapers, establishing the first Nigerian political party, and using his powerful oratory to unite Nigerians against colonial oppression.
translatedText: Ya dage da kafa jaridu, ya kafa jam’iyyar siyasa ta Najeriya ta farko, da kuma amfani da kakkausan lafazinsa wajen hada kan ‘yan Nijeriya a kan zaluncin ‘yan mulkin mallaka.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Tsakiyar Karni na 20

sourceText: Hendrik Witbooi
translatedText: Hendrik Witbooi

sourceText: The anguish of foreign invasion, internal tribal conflicts, and the ultimate struggle against colonial subjugation.
translatedText: Bacin rai na mamayewar kasashen waje, rikice-rikicen kabilanci na cikin gida, da gwagwarmayar karshe na yaki da mulkin mallaka.

sourceText: By uniting disparate Namibian peoples against a common enemy, turning from a tactical ally of the Germans to their most determined opponent for freedom.
translatedText: Ta hanyar hada kan al'ummar Namibiya masu rarrabuwar kawuna a kan makiya daya, da suka juya daga dabarar kawancen Jamusawa zuwa ga babban abokin hamayyarsu na neman 'yanci.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Huda Sha'arawi
translatedText: Huda Sha'arawi

sourceText: A secluded harem life from childhood, an early arranged marriage, and the societal and legal restrictions placed upon women.
translatedText: Rayuwar haramtacciyar rayuwa tun tana kuruciya, daurin aure da wuri, da hani da al'umma da shari'a suka sanya mata.

sourceText: Self-empowerment through education and reading, founding the Egyptian Feminist Union by uniting with other women, and leading public acts of defiance to create change.
translatedText: Ƙarfafa kai ta hanyar ilimi da karatu, kafa ƙungiyar mata ta Masar ta hanyar haɗa kai da sauran mata, da jagorantar ayyukan rashin amincewa da jama'a don haifar da canji.

sourceText: Early 20th Century
translatedText: Farkon Karni na 20

sourceText: Deep inner conflict and anguish from a hedonistic youth, adherence to Manichaeism, and the early death of his son.
translatedText: Rikici mai zurfi da bacin rai daga matashin hedonistic, riko da Manichaeism, da farkon mutuwar ɗansa.

sourceText: Converted to Christianity through his mother Monica's prayers and Bishop Ambrose's sermons, finding intellectual and spiritual answers in God's grace by dedicating his life to theology and philosophy.
translatedText: Ya tuba zuwa Kiristanci ta wurin addu'o'in mahaifiyarsa Monica da wa'azin Bishop Ambrose, samun amsoshi na hankali da na ruhaniya cikin alherin Allah ta wurin sadaukar da rayuwarsa ga tiyoloji da falsafa.

sourceText: Late Roman Empire
translatedText: Late Roman Empire

sourceText: J. E. Casely Hayford
translatedText: J. E. Casely Hayford

sourceText: Witnessing the erosion of traditional African institutions and rights under colonial rule.
translatedText: Shaidawa rugujewar cibiyoyi da hakkoki na gargajiya na Afirka a ƙarƙashin mulkin mallaka.

sourceText: Advocating for African self-determination through political organization, journalism, and literature.
translatedText: Ba da shawarwari ga cin gashin kai na Afirka ta hanyar ƙungiyoyin siyasa, aikin jarida, da adabi.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Frantz Fanon
translatedText: Frantz Fanon

sourceText: He grappled with the internalised racism and identity crises born from a colonial upbringing, and was profoundly traumatized by the brutality he witnessed as a psychiatrist during the Algerian War.
translatedText: Ya yi fama da rikice-rikicen wariyar launin fata na cikin gida da kuma rikice-rikicen asali da aka haifa daga tarbiyyar turawan mulkin mallaka, kuma ya damu matuka da rashin tausayin da ya gani a matsayin likitan hauka a lokacin yakin Aljeriya.

sourceText: He channeled his experiences into his psychoanalytic practice and revolutionary writings, theorizing the path to decolonization and advocating for the liberation of oppressed peoples worldwide.
translatedText: Ya gabatar da abubuwan da ya faru a cikin ayyukansa na tunani da kuma rubuce-rubuce na juyin juya hali, yana ba da ra'ayi na hanyar kawar da mulkin mallaka da bayar da shawarwari don 'yantar da mutanen da ake zalunta a duniya.

sourceText: 20th Century
translatedText: Karni na 20

sourceText: W. E. B. Du Bois
translatedText: W.E.B. Du Bois

sourceText: The pervasive 'color line' of racial discrimination and the social and intellectual isolation it created. He also faced opposition from both white society and other Black leaders.
translatedText: Yaɗuwar 'launi' na wariyar launin fata da keɓancewar zamantakewa da tunani da ya haifar. Ya kuma fuskanci adawa daga al'ummar farar fata da sauran shugabannin bakaken fata.

sourceText: Through rigorous scholarship, prolific writing, and tireless activism, he confronted racial injustice, co-founding the NAACP and championing Pan-Africanism to unite people of African descent globally.
translatedText: Ta hanyar ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun ƙwararrun mutane, ya fuskanci rashin adalci na launin fata, tare da kafa NAACP da kuma jagorancin Pan-Africanism don haɗakar da mutanen Afirka a duniya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Mid 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Tsakiyar Karni na 20

sourceText: George Gemistos Plethon
translatedText: George Gemistos Plethon

sourceText: Disillusionment with the declining Byzantine Empire and the rigid doctrines of the Orthodox Church.
translatedText: Rashin jin daɗi tare da raguwar daular Byzantine da kuma tsayayyen koyaswar Cocin Orthodox.

sourceText: Reviving ancient Greek philosophy, especially Plato's thought, to propose a new social and political model.
translatedText: Farfado da tsohuwar falsafar Girka, musamman tunanin Plato, don ba da shawarar sabon salon zamantakewa da siyasa.

sourceText: 15th Century Byzantine Empire
translatedText: Daular Byzantine ta Karni na 15

sourceText: Nikolai Gogol
translatedText: Nikolai Gogol

sourceText: Profound religious turmoil, mental instability, and a crippling self-doubt about the moral purpose of his own literary creations.
translatedText: Zurfafa rikice-rikice na addini, rashin kwanciyar hankali, da gurgunta shakku game da manufar ɗabi'a na abubuwan da ya halitta na adabi.

sourceText: He channeled his anguish into satirical masterpieces, but ultimately succumbed to religious fanaticism and self-destructive asceticism in his final years.
translatedText: Ya ba da ɓacin ransa zuwa ƙwararrun zane-zane na satirical, amma a ƙarshe ya faɗa cikin tsattsauran ra'ayi na addini da ruɗin kai a cikin shekarunsa na ƙarshe.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Nikolay Muravyov-Amursky
translatedText: Nikolay Muravov-Amursky

sourceText: Facing bureaucratic inertia from St. Petersburg, the immense logistical challenges of a vast and undeveloped territory, and the delicate diplomatic dance with a weakened but still formidable Qing China.
translatedText: Fuskantar inertia na hukuma daga St.

sourceText: By acting with decisive, often unilateral, authority granted by the Tsar, and by fostering a spirit of pioneering and settlement, I pushed through my vision for a Russian Pacific.
translatedText: Ta yin aiki tare da yanke hukunci, sau da yawa ba tare da izini ba, ikon da Tsar ya ba da, da kuma haɓaka ruhun majagaba da sasantawa, na matsa cikin hangen nesa na game da yankin Pacific na Rasha.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Nikolai Vavilov
translatedText: Nikolai Vavilov

sourceText: Persecuted and imprisoned for his scientific beliefs, which contradicted the state-sponsored theories of Trofim Lysenko.
translatedText: An tsananta masa da kuma ɗaure shi saboda imaninsa na kimiyya, wanda ya saba wa ka'idodin da gwamnati ta dauki nauyin Trofim Lysenko.

sourceText: His legacy was posthumously restored, and his work on crop diversity and seed banking is now considered fundamental to global food security.
translatedText: An sake dawo da gadonsa bayan mutuwarsa, kuma aikinsa a kan bambancin amfanin gona da bankin iri yanzu ana daukarsa yana da mahimmanci ga samar da abinci a duniya.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Nikolai Przhevalsky
translatedText: Nikolai Przhevalsky

sourceText: Battling harsh terrain, extreme weather, hostility from local populations, and diseases contracted during his expeditions.
translatedText: Yin yaƙi da yanayi mai tsauri, matsanancin yanayi, ƙiyayya daga jama'ar yankin, da cututtuka da aka kama a lokacin balaguron sa.

sourceText: Overcame these challenges through sheer willpower, meticulous planning, and an unyielding passion for discovery and the unknown.
translatedText: Na shawo kan waɗannan ƙalubalen ta hanyar ƙwaƙƙwaran iƙirari, tsare-tsare mai zurfi, da sha'awar ganowa da abin da ba a sani ba.

sourceText: 19th Century
translatedText: Karni na 19

sourceText: Danylo Halytskyi
translatedText: Danylo Halytsky

sourceText: Exile in his youth after his father's death and constant rebellions from internal boyars who opposed the unification of his lands.
translatedText: Ƙaura a cikin ƙuruciyarsa bayan mutuwar mahaifinsa da kuma tawaye na yau da kullum daga boyars na ciki waɗanda suka yi adawa da haɗin kai na ƙasashensa.

sourceText: Through relentless military campaigns and shrewd diplomacy, he defeated his rivals, balanced the powers of the Mongols and Western Europe, and was crowned king.
translatedText: Ta hanyar kamfen na soja da basirar diflomasiyya, ya ci nasara a kan abokan hamayyarsa, ya daidaita ikon Mongols da Yammacin Turai, ya kuma nada shi sarki.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 13

sourceText: David IV of Georgia
translatedText: David IV na Georgia

sourceText: Inheriting a kingdom ravaged by Seljuk Turk invasions and facing constant rebellions from powerful feudal lords.
translatedText: Gadon daular da Seljuk Turk ta mamaye da kuma fuskantar tawaye akai-akai daga manyan sarakunan fada.

sourceText: Through sweeping military, administrative, and church reforms, and a decisive victory at the Battle of Didgori.
translatedText: Ta hanyar manyan sojoji, gudanarwa, da gyare-gyare na coci, da gagarumin nasara a yakin Didgori.

sourceText: 11th-12th Century
translatedText: Karni na 11-12

sourceText: Rabban Bar Sauma
translatedText: Rabban Bar Sauma

sourceText: Unable to complete his original spiritual pilgrimage to Jerusalem due to political turmoil, he was thrust into the world of international diplomacy.
translatedText: Ya kasa kammala aikin hajjinsa na ruhaniya na asali zuwa Kudus saboda rudanin siyasa, an jefa shi cikin duniyar diflomasiyya ta kasa da kasa.

sourceText: He embraced his new role as a divine mission, dedicating himself to fostering an alliance between East and West and documenting his extraordinary travels.
translatedText: Ya rungumi sabon aikinsa a matsayin aikin Allah, yana sadaukar da kansa don haɓaka ƙawance tsakanin Gabas da Yamma da rubuta tafiye-tafiyensa na ban mamaki.

sourceText: 13th Century
translatedText: Karni na 13

sourceText: Lesya Ukrainka
translatedText: Lesya Ukrainka

sourceText: A lifelong battle with bone tuberculosis, causing constant pain and limiting mobility, alongside the political oppression of Ukrainian culture by the Russian Empire.
translatedText: Yaƙi na rayuwa tare da cutar tarin fuka, yana haifar da ciwo akai-akai da iyakance motsi, tare da zalunci na siyasa na al'adun Ukrainian da Daular Rasha ta yi.

sourceText: She channeled her physical and emotional suffering into powerful literary works, becoming a symbol of Ukrainian resilience. Her extensive travels for medical treatment also broadened her intellectual horizons, enriching her writing.
translatedText: Ta ƙaddamar da wahalar ta jiki da ta rai cikin ayyukan adabi masu ƙarfi, ta zama alama ce ta juriyar Yukren. Yawan tafiye-tafiyen da ta yi don neman magani ya kuma faɗaɗa tunaninta, wanda ya haɓaka rubuce-rubucenta.

sourceText: Late 19th - Early 20th Century
translatedText: Marigayi 19th - Farkon ƙarni na 20

sourceText: Despite immense fame, I was blinded and cast out from the court in my old age, dying in poverty and obscurity.
translatedText: Duk da shaharar da aka yi, an makantar da ni, an kore ni daga kotu da tsufana, ina mutuwa cikin talauci da duhu.

sourceText: Though my life ended in tragedy, my poetry survived, laying the foundation for all Persian literature to come and earning me the title 'Adam of Poets'.
translatedText: Ko da yake rayuwata ta ƙare a cikin bala’i, amma waƙara ta tsira, ta kafa harsashin duk littattafan Farisa su zo suka sa ni suna ‘Adamu mawaƙa’.

sourceText: 10th Century Samanid Empire
translatedText: Daular Samanid ta Karni na 10

sourceText: Mahmud al-Kashgari
translatedText: Mahmud al-Kashgari

sourceText: Forced into exile from my homeland due to political upheaval and witnessing the gradual decline of Turkic cultural prominence in favor of Arabic.
translatedText: An tilasta wa yin hijira daga ƙasara ta haihuwa saboda tashe-tashen hankula na siyasa da kuma ganin yadda shaharar al'adun Turkawa ke raguwa a hankali don neman Larabci.

sourceText: I transformed my exile into a grand ethnographic journey, traveling across Turkic lands to meticulously document their diverse languages, customs, and poetry, culminating in my magnum opus.
translatedText: Na mayar da gudun hijira na zuwa wata babbar tafiya ta ƙabilanci, na bi ta cikin ƙasashen Turkawa don yin taka-tsantsan da yarensu, al'adunsu, da kuma waƙoƙinsu dabam-dabam, wanda ya kai ga girma na.
